scheduledEvents.name: Worker stale-task detection
scheduledEvents.description: Planned Mission Control automation using scripts/mission_control_stale_task_detector.py to flag in-progress agent tasks with no activity for more than 60 minutes and route them into the recovery flow.

scheduledEvents.name: Mission Control state hygiene review
scheduledEvents.description: Operational review of open bugs, docs and memories coverage, demo residue, and persistence trust baseline before relying on Mission Control more heavily.

projects.name: Njuskalo Smartphone Deal Radar
projects.description: A smartphone deal radar focused on Njuskalo listings, with Telegram alerting and a mobile-API-first acquisition strategy.
projects.goal: Provide Marko with a dependable way to discover underpriced or otherwise high-opportunity smartphone listings on Njuskalo quickly enough to act before the best deals disappear.
projects.desiredOutcome: A private, Dockerized system that continuously monitors Njuskalo smartphone listings, normalizes and deduplicates them, applies clear deal-selection logic, and sends concise Telegram alerts only when a listing looks meaningfully actionable.
successCriteria: A realistic Njuskalo acquisition path is validated and can fetch fresh listings consistently enough for ongoing polling.
The system detects new target listings with normal end-to-end alert latency measured in minutes, not hours.
Telegram alerts are concise, actionable, and trusted because most alerts are plausibly worth opening immediately.
Duplicate alerts for unchanged listings are rare and repost/update handling is acceptable for daily use.
State survives container restarts and preserves seen-listing / alert history needed for deduplication.
constraints: Keep the repository and all artifacts private in GitHub.
Prefer mobile/API-like access before HTML or browser fallback, but allow architecture changes if feasibility work proves a better path.
Keep v1 strictly focused on smartphone detection and Telegram alerting.
Minimize request volume and anti-bot risk; reliability is more important than aggressive coverage.
Do not start implementation until the required proof points are validated.
assumptions: Njuskalo exposes at least one practical retrieval path for recent listings.
Listing data includes enough stable identifiers or field combinations for deduplication.
Telegram is the only required notification channel for v1.
Single-user operation is acceptable for the first version.
Initial deal quality can be driven by rules and lightweight heuristics rather than heavy ML.
projects.planningNotes: Scope doc saved as "Njuskalo Smartphone Deal Radar - Product Scope v1". Implementation is blocked on proof of access path, field coverage, deduplication strategy, detail-enrichment feasibility, first usable deal logic, and alert usefulness. v1 boundary: smartphone-only detection and Telegram alerts; no seller outreach, purchase automation, or broad marketplace coverage.

conversationMemories.title: Mission Control operational baseline populated
conversationMemories.content: Replaced obvious demo residue in the targeted sections with real operating context. Added docs for the operating model, worker rules, persistence baseline, and projects model; registered Misko as orchestrator; set the mission statement; added durable long-term memories; and created only justified draft calendar entries. Tools were left empty intentionally because there are no real Mission Control-native tool definitions to register yet. Njuskalo implementation work was not started.

longTermMemories.title: Marko prefers direct, practical communication
longTermMemories.content: Call him Marko. Keep communication concise, direct, and practical. Default to high thinking for real work.

longTermMemories.title: Mission Control is the source of truth
longTermMemories.content: Important operational work should be reflected in Mission Control while it is happening. Tasks, docs, memories, bugs, calendar items, and agent state should not live only in chat.

longTermMemories.title: Misko operates as orchestrator
longTermMemories.content: Misko coordinates work, delegates bounded execution to subagents, and reviews outputs. Normal worker target is 6, hard max 8. Heavy coding should go through Claude Code with plan-first review.

longTermMemories.title: Persistence truth model
longTermMemories.content: The durable store wins over runtime or cache state. False emptiness after restart counts as a real failure. Saved should mean durably committed and reloadable after restart or rebuild.

longTermMemories.title: GitHub work must remain private
longTermMemories.content: All repositories and GitHub activity for this workspace should remain private.

docs.title: Model Configuration — Permanent Rule (2026-04-03) [UPDATED]
docs.content:
# Model Configuration

**Date:** 2026-04-03

## Rule
All tasks use MiniMax-M2.7 with thinking: high — no exceptions.

## Model Routing
- **MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed:** short, simple, quick tasks (lookups, formatting, trivial edits)
- **MiniMax-M2.7 (standard/high):** anything needing real reasoning
- Use judgment — if it can be done in one pass without deep reasoning, highspeed is fine

## Details
- Worker model: MiniMax-M2.7, thinking: high
- No Claude Code available
- No GPT-5.4 routing — limit reached, workers always use MiniMax-M2.7

## Updated files
- AGENTS.md
- TOOLS.md
- MEMORY.md

team.missionStatement: Use Mission Control as Marko's durable operating layer: keep tasks, docs, memories, bugs, calendar, projects, and agent state aligned so execution is reviewable, reliable, and never lives only in chat.

agents.name: Misko
agents.description: Primary OpenClaw orchestrator for Marko. Keeps Mission Control as source of truth, delegates bounded work to workers, and reviews plans and results.

bugs.title: PUT /api/docs/{id} returns 404 — doc update endpoint missing
notes: PUT /api/docs/{id} now works after MC restart/rebuild
